item.title: ViiV Healthcare Positive Action Funding Opportunities: Up to £300,000 Available for Community-Led HIV and AIDS Initiatives Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Organizations working to eliminate AIDS and improve the lives of people living with HIV have an opportunity to access significant funding through the ViiV Healthcare Positive Action Programme. The initiative supports community-led and community-based organizations that are developing innovative solutions and scaling proven interventions that address HIV prevention, testing, treatment, care, and long-term support. Established […]

The post ViiV Healthcare Positive Action Funding Opportunities: Up to £300,000 Available for Community-Led HIV and AIDS Initiatives Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations working to eliminate AIDS and improve the lives of people living with HIV have an opportunity to access significant funding through the ViiV Healthcare Positive Action Programme. The initiative supports community-led and community-based organizations that are developing innovative solutions and scaling proven interventions that address HIV prevention, testing, treatment, care, and long-term support.

Established as part of ViiV Healthcare’s commitment to ending HIV and ensuring that no person living with HIV is left behind, Positive Action has become one of the world’s leading funding mechanisms for community organizations responding to HIV and AIDS. Over the years, the program has supported hundreds of organizations and projects across more than 100 countries, helping communities address HIV-related challenges through locally led solutions.

About the Positive Action Programme

Positive Action was created to strengthen the HIV response by investing directly in community organizations that work with populations most affected by HIV. The programme prioritizes community leadership, local ownership, and sustainable solutions that can create long-term impact.

The initiative supports organizations that:

  • Improve HIV prevention services.
  • Increase HIV testing and diagnosis.
  • Enhance access to treatment and care.
  • Address HIV-related stigma and discrimination.
  • Support vulnerable and marginalized populations.
  • Strengthen community systems and local leadership.
  • Improve health outcomes for people living with HIV.

Funding Streams Available

The Positive Action Programme currently offers two primary funding streams designed to support organizations at different stages of program development.

1. Innovator Funding

Innovator Funding is intended for organizations seeking to test and pilot new approaches to HIV-related challenges.

Funding Available:

  • Up to £100,000 over two years.
  • Maximum of £50,000 per year.

Key Features:

  • Supports testing and piloting of innovative approaches.
  • The proposed intervention must be new to the applicant organization.
  • The approach may already have been implemented successfully elsewhere.
  • Designed to generate evidence before a larger-scale rollout.
  • Focuses on experimentation, learning, and innovation.

2. Momentum Funding

Momentum Funding supports organizations that already have evidence-based interventions and want to expand or scale them.

Funding Available:

  • Up to £300,000 over three years.
  • Maximum of £100,000 per year.

Key Features:

  • Supports expansion of proven HIV interventions.
  • Applicants must demonstrate previous implementation experience.
  • Organizations must provide evidence that the intervention works.
  • Funding can build on previous investments from Positive Action or other donors.
  • Emphasizes sustainability beyond the grant period.

What Types of Projects Are Supported?

Positive Action seeks projects that create measurable impact within communities affected by HIV and AIDS.

Examples of supported interventions include:

  1. HIV prevention programs.
  2. HIV testing and diagnosis initiatives.
  3. Linkage-to-care projects.
  4. Treatment adherence support.
  5. Community health worker programs.
  6. HIV stigma reduction campaigns.
  7. Mental health support services.
  8. Programs supporting women and girls.
  9. Youth-focused HIV interventions.
  10. Services for marginalized and vulnerable populations.
  11. Community-led advocacy initiatives.
  12. Capacity-building programs for local organizations.

Recent funding rounds have focused on:

  • Paediatric HIV.
  • Adolescent HIV prevention and care.
  • Caregiver support programs.
  • HIV testing and treatment access.
  • Community-led health interventions.
  • Scaling evidence-informed HIV solutions.

Why This Funding Matters

Community organizations often understand local challenges better than large institutions. Positive Action recognizes that sustainable progress against HIV requires solutions that are designed and led by affected communities themselves.

The programme therefore prioritizes:

  • Community leadership.
  • Local ownership.
  • Equity in partnerships.
  • Sustainable development.
  • Capacity strengthening.
  • Long-term impact.

Beyond financial support, Positive Action also offers opportunities for organizational development, learning exchanges, networking, and access to technical expertise that can help grantees strengthen their impact and sustainability.

Additional Support Available

Organizations funded through Positive Action may benefit from:

  • Organizational development support.
  • Learning and networking opportunities.
  • Access to skilled volunteers.
  • Knowledge-sharing platforms.
  • Community programming guidance.
  • Strategic partnerships.

Application Process

Applications are submitted through the Positive Action online grants portal. Organizations interested in future funding opportunities are encouraged to monitor the official funding opportunities page regularly for announcements regarding new funding rounds and application windows.

Deadline

As of May 2026, the official Positive Action Funding Opportunities page indicates that there are currently no open funding rounds. Interested organizations should continue checking the official website for upcoming calls for proposals and application deadlines.

Final Thoughts

The ViiV Healthcare Positive Action Programme remains one of the most impactful global funding initiatives dedicated to supporting community-led responses to HIV and AIDS. With grants of up to £300,000 available through its Momentum Funding stream and up to £100,000 through Innovator Funding, the programme offers a valuable opportunity for organizations seeking to create sustainable, community-driven change.

Whether your organization is piloting a new idea or scaling a proven intervention, Positive Action provides both financial resources and strategic support to help communities move closer to the goal of ending AIDS and improving outcomes for people living with HIV worldwide.

Discover more global grants opportunities on OFY here

The post ViiV Healthcare Positive Action Funding Opportunities: Up to £300,000 Available for Community-Led HIV and AIDS Initiatives Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: School Enterprise Challenge 2026: Free Global Entrepreneurship Competition for Schools with International Recognition, Prizes, and Real Business Experience
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Schools around the world are being invited to participate in the School Enterprise Challenge 2026, a free international entrepreneurship programme that equips students with practical business skills by helping them create, launch, and manage real school-based businesses. Designed to bridge the gap between classroom learning and real-world experience, the programme has become one of the […]

The post School Enterprise Challenge 2026: Free Global Entrepreneurship Competition for Schools with International Recognition, Prizes, and Real Business Experience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Schools around the world are being invited to participate in the School Enterprise Challenge 2026, a free international entrepreneurship programme that equips students with practical business skills by helping them create, launch, and manage real school-based businesses. Designed to bridge the gap between classroom learning and real-world experience, the programme has become one of the world’s leading school entrepreneurship initiatives, empowering hundreds of thousands of young people globally.

The School Enterprise Challenge is organized by Teach A Man To Fish and is open to schools worldwide, from pre-primary to secondary level. Through a structured, step-by-step learning approach, students gain hands-on experience in entrepreneurship while developing critical life and leadership skills that prepare them for future careers and business opportunities.

What is the School Enterprise Challenge?

The School Enterprise Challenge is a free international education and entrepreneurship programme that supports teachers and students in establishing sustainable school businesses. Rather than learning entrepreneurship through theory alone, students actively participate in developing business ideas, creating business plans, launching enterprises, managing finances, marketing products or services, and evaluating business performance.

Since its launch in 2011, the programme has benefited hundreds of thousands of young people worldwide and has become a recognized platform for promoting entrepreneurial thinking among school-aged learners. Schools from numerous countries have participated, creating enterprises ranging from agricultural projects and food production businesses to crafts, technology ventures, and environmental enterprises.

Why the School Enterprise Challenge Matters

Traditional education often focuses on academic knowledge while providing limited exposure to practical business and entrepreneurship skills. The School Enterprise Challenge addresses this gap by introducing experiential learning opportunities that allow students to learn through action.

The programme helps learners develop:

  1. Entrepreneurial skills
  2. Financial literacy
  3. Leadership abilities
  4. Teamwork and collaboration
  5. Problem-solving skills
  6. Creativity and innovation
  7. Communication skills
  8. Business planning capabilities
  9. Decision-making skills
  10. Confidence and resilience

Students are encouraged to identify opportunities within their communities, design solutions, and create sustainable business models that generate real income while creating positive social impact.

Benefits for Students

Participating students receive a unique opportunity to experience entrepreneurship firsthand.

Key benefits include:

1. Real Business Experience

Students do not simply study entrepreneurship; they operate actual businesses. This practical exposure enables them to understand how businesses function in real-world environments.

2. Development of Future-Ready Skills

The programme focuses on essential 21st-century competencies that employers and universities increasingly value.

These include:

  • Leadership
  • Communication
  • Critical thinking
  • Innovation
  • Collaboration
  • Financial management
  • Project management
  • Strategic planning

3. Improved Academic Performance

Research conducted on programme participants has shown positive educational outcomes. Students who participated were found to be significantly more likely to achieve top grades in mathematics and science compared to non-participants.

4. International Exposure

Students become part of a global network of entrepreneurial schools and learners, allowing them to exchange ideas and learn from successful school businesses operating in different countries.

Benefits for Teachers

The School Enterprise Challenge is not only beneficial to students but also provides valuable professional development opportunities for educators.

Teachers gain:

  • Access to entrepreneurship education resources.
  • Ready-to-use teaching materials.
  • Practical business education guides.
  • Experiential learning methodologies.
  • Membership in an international educator network.
  • Opportunities to strengthen classroom engagement.
  • Exposure to innovative teaching techniques.

The programme enables teachers to bring classroom concepts to life through practical application and student-led learning experiences.

Benefits for Schools

Participating schools can benefit in multiple ways beyond educational outcomes.

Schools may:

  • Generate additional income through student-run businesses.
  • Support school improvement projects.
  • Enhance entrepreneurship education.
  • Strengthen community engagement.
  • Improve student retention and participation.
  • Build a culture of innovation and creativity.
  • Increase opportunities for practical learning.

Many schools have successfully reinvested profits from their businesses into educational resources, infrastructure improvements, and community development projects.

How the Programme Works

The School Enterprise Challenge follows a structured curriculum that guides schools through every stage of the business development process.

Participants learn how to:

  1. Identify business opportunities.
  2. Conduct market research.
  3. Generate business ideas.
  4. Develop business plans.
  5. Create financial projections.
  6. Launch school businesses.
  7. Manage operations.
  8. Monitor performance.
  9. Prepare annual reports.
  10. Scale successful enterprises.

The programme’s comprehensive framework helps students understand every aspect of entrepreneurship from idea generation to implementation.

Global Impact of the School Enterprise Challenge

The programme has demonstrated remarkable success across diverse educational settings worldwide.

Examples of successful student-led enterprises include:

  • Agricultural ventures.
  • Food production businesses.
  • School shops.
  • Environmental projects.
  • Community service enterprises.
  • Manufacturing initiatives.
  • Technology-based ventures.

Many participating schools have reported increased student engagement, improved learning outcomes, and stronger entrepreneurial mindsets among learners.

Registration and Participation

The School Enterprise Challenge 2026 is now open for registration. Schools can join free of charge and gain access to the online platform, educational resources, training materials, and guidance needed to develop successful student-led businesses. The programme is available in both English and Spanish, making it accessible to a broad international audience.

Deadline

The School Enterprise Challenge allows schools to register throughout the year. Specific submission dates for business ideas, business plans, and annual reports may vary depending on the competition cycle. Interested schools are encouraged to register early and regularly check the official website for updates regarding competition timelines and award deadlines.

Final Thoughts

The School Enterprise Challenge 2026 represents an outstanding opportunity for schools to introduce entrepreneurship into education through practical, hands-on learning. By enabling students to build and operate real businesses, the programme equips young people with the knowledge, confidence, and skills needed to thrive in an increasingly competitive and innovation-driven world.

For schools seeking to inspire the next generation of entrepreneurs, leaders, and problem-solvers, the School Enterprise Challenge offers a proven, globally recognized platform that combines education, entrepreneurship, and community impact into one transformative experience.

Discover more global competition opportunities on OFY here

The post School Enterprise Challenge 2026: Free Global Entrepreneurship Competition for Schools with International Recognition, Prizes, and Real Business Experience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNESCO Associate Project Officer – Research Job in India 2026: Join a Leading UN Agency Driving Innovation in Education Research
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Professionals interested in educational research, social and emotional learning, data-driven policy development, and international development now have an exciting opportunity to work with one of the world’s most respected international organizations. The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) is currently recruiting an Associate Project Officer – Research based in New Delhi, India. This […]

The post UNESCO Associate Project Officer – Research Job in India 2026: Join a Leading UN Agency Driving Innovation in Education Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Professionals interested in educational research, social and emotional learning, data-driven policy development, and international development now have an exciting opportunity to work with one of the world’s most respected international organizations. The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) is currently recruiting an Associate Project Officer – Research based in New Delhi, India.

This position offers qualified professionals the opportunity to contribute to cutting-edge educational research while supporting UNESCO’s mission of building peace through education, science, culture, and communication. The role is hosted within UNESCO’s Education Sector and provides an opportunity to work on innovative research initiatives that influence education systems and policy development.

About UNESCO

The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) is a specialized agency of the United Nations founded in 1945. UNESCO works across the globe to promote international cooperation in education, science, culture, and communication with the goal of fostering peace, sustainable development, and human rights. The organization operates in more than 190 member states and collaborates with governments, institutions, researchers, and civil society organizations worldwide.

Working with UNESCO provides professionals with the opportunity to contribute to meaningful global initiatives while gaining valuable experience within the United Nations system.

Overview of the Associate Project Officer – Research Position

The Associate Project Officer – Research position is a Service Contract role at the SC8 level and is based in New Delhi, India. The successful candidate will work within UNESCO’s Education Sector and contribute to research activities that support evidence-based educational programming and policy development.

Key Job Details

  • Position Title: Associate Project Officer – Research
  • Organization: UNESCO
  • Department: Education Sector (ED)
  • Duty Station: New Delhi, India
  • Contract Type: Service Contract
  • Grade Level: SC8
  • Work Arrangement: On-site
  • Contract Duration: 12 months
  • Extension Possibility: Subject to funding availability and satisfactory performance
  • Recruitment Type: External Candidates
  • Application Mode: Online Application through UNESCO Careers Portal

What Will the Successful Candidate Do?

The Associate Project Officer – Research will support research activities within UNESCO’s education programmes, particularly those focused on generating evidence that can strengthen educational policy and practice.

Responsibilities may include:

1. Research Design and Development

The selected candidate will help design and implement research studies that contribute to understanding educational challenges and opportunities.

Tasks may involve:

  • Designing research frameworks.
  • Developing research methodologies.
  • Supporting data collection processes.
  • Conducting literature reviews.
  • Preparing research proposals.
  • Contributing to evidence-based educational initiatives.

2. Data Analysis and Evidence Generation

Research officers are expected to analyze data and generate findings that inform educational programming and policy.

This may include:

  • Quantitative analysis.
  • Qualitative analysis.
  • Research reporting.
  • Policy briefs.
  • Educational impact assessments.
  • Monitoring and evaluation activities.

3. Stakeholder Engagement

The role requires engagement with various stakeholders, including:

  • Educational institutions.
  • Government agencies.
  • Researchers.
  • Development partners.
  • International organizations.
  • Project beneficiaries.

4. Knowledge Management and Reporting

The selected professional will support the preparation of:

  • Research reports.
  • Technical publications.
  • Knowledge products.
  • Project documentation.
  • Presentations and dissemination materials.

Why This Job is a Major Career Opportunity

Working for UNESCO offers far more than a regular research position.

Benefits include:

International Recognition

UNESCO is one of the most respected organizations in the world. Experience gained within UNESCO significantly enhances a professional’s international profile.

Exposure to Global Development Work

Employees gain direct experience working on international education and development projects that influence policy and practice across multiple countries.

Professional Growth

The position provides opportunities to:

  • Work with leading researchers.
  • Collaborate with policymakers.
  • Develop advanced research skills.
  • Build international networks.
  • Gain United Nations experience.

Potential Contract Extension

The initial appointment is for one year, but UNESCO has indicated that the contract may be extended based on performance and available funding.

Qualifications and Skills UNESCO is Looking For

UNESCO generally seeks candidates with strong academic and professional backgrounds.

Preferred qualifications include:

Educational Requirements

Applicants are typically expected to possess:

  • A Master’s degree or equivalent advanced university qualification.
  • Academic specialization in education, psychology, social sciences, cognitive science, public policy, research methods, international development, or related fields.

Professional Experience

Candidates should demonstrate:

  • Relevant research experience.
  • Experience conducting data analysis.
  • Experience supporting research projects.
  • Experience preparing technical reports.
  • Familiarity with evidence-based policy development.
  • Experience working with multidisciplinary teams.

Technical Skills

Strong candidates are expected to possess:

  • Research design expertise.
  • Analytical thinking skills.
  • Statistical analysis capabilities.
  • Report writing skills.
  • Communication skills.
  • Project management abilities.

Language Requirements

Excellent English communication skills are essential for this role. Knowledge of additional languages may be considered an advantage.

Important Eligibility Condition

One of the most important requirements highlighted in the vacancy announcement is that only Indian nationals are eligible to apply for this position. Applicants must therefore hold Indian nationality before submitting an application.

UNESCO Core Values

Applicants are expected to demonstrate alignment with UNESCO’s core values:

  • Commitment to the Organization.
  • Integrity.
  • Respect for Diversity.
  • Professionalism.

These values play a significant role during the recruitment and selection process.

Application Deadline

Interested applicants must submit their applications before:

30 June 2026 (Midnight India Standard Time – IST). Applications submitted after the deadline are unlikely to be considered.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted through UNESCO’s official careers portal. Candidates should prepare:

  1. Updated CV or résumé.
  2. Academic certificates.
  3. Professional references.
  4. Supporting documents demonstrating qualifications and experience.

Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible to avoid last-minute technical issues. UNESCO may use written assessments, competency-based interviews, and reference checks as part of the selection process.

Final Thoughts

The UNESCO Associate Project Officer – Research position in New Delhi represents an outstanding opportunity for research professionals seeking to contribute to global education and development. With direct involvement in educational research, policy support, and evidence generation, the role offers meaningful work, international exposure, and the chance to be part of the United Nations system.

For qualified Indian professionals passionate about education, research, and international development, this vacancy provides an excellent platform to build a rewarding career while contributing to UNESCO’s mission of creating a more peaceful, inclusive, and sustainable world through education.

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY here

The post UNESCO Associate Project Officer – Research Job in India 2026: Join a Leading UN Agency Driving Innovation in Education Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ILO Seeks External Expert to Review Global Entrepreneurship Ecosystems Programming: International Consultancy Opportunity for MSME and Enterprise Development Specialists
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The International Labour Organization (ILO) is inviting applications from qualified experts and consultants for an important international assignment under its Enterprises Department. The opportunity, titled “Call for External Collaborator/Expert for the Review of ILO Entrepreneurship Ecosystems Programming,” seeks a highly qualified professional to conduct a comprehensive review of the ILO’s entrepreneurship ecosystems interventions and programming […]

The post ILO Seeks External Expert to Review Global Entrepreneurship Ecosystems Programming: International Consultancy Opportunity for MSME and Enterprise Development Specialists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Labour Organization (ILO) is inviting applications from qualified experts and consultants for an important international assignment under its Enterprises Department. The opportunity, titled “Call for External Collaborator/Expert for the Review of ILO Entrepreneurship Ecosystems Programming,” seeks a highly qualified professional to conduct a comprehensive review of the ILO’s entrepreneurship ecosystems interventions and programming within the framework of its Enterprise Development and Micro, Small and Medium Enterprise (MSME) initiatives.

This consultancy presents a unique opportunity for experienced entrepreneurship development professionals, researchers, policy experts, and enterprise development specialists to collaborate with one of the world’s leading international organizations dedicated to promoting decent work, sustainable enterprises, and inclusive economic growth.

The review will play a critical role in assessing the effectiveness, relevance, sustainability, and impact of the ILO’s entrepreneurship ecosystem interventions while identifying opportunities for future improvements and strategic direction.

About the International Labour Organization (ILO)

The International Labour Organization is a specialized agency of the United Nations that promotes social justice, internationally recognized labour rights, decent employment opportunities, social protection, and social dialogue. Established in 1919, the ILO works with governments, employers, and workers across the globe to foster sustainable economic growth and improve working conditions.

One of the ILO’s major priorities is supporting entrepreneurship and enterprise development as key drivers of employment creation, innovation, economic inclusion, and poverty reduction.

Through its Enterprises Department and MSME-focused initiatives, the ILO supports governments, development partners, business support organizations, and entrepreneurs in creating enabling environments that foster business growth and job creation.

Understanding Entrepreneurship Ecosystems

Entrepreneurship ecosystems refer to the interconnected network of institutions, policies, organizations, investors, educational institutions, support services, and entrepreneurs that collectively influence entrepreneurial success within a country or region.

Strong entrepreneurship ecosystems contribute to:

  • Business creation and growth.
  • Innovation and competitiveness.
  • Employment generation.
  • Economic diversification.
  • Increased productivity.
  • Youth entrepreneurship development.
  • Women’s economic empowerment.
  • Sustainable enterprise development.

The ILO has invested significantly in strengthening entrepreneurship ecosystems across different countries and regions through technical assistance, policy support, capacity building, training programmes, and enterprise development initiatives.

Purpose of the Review

The selected external collaborator or expert will undertake a comprehensive assessment of the ILO’s entrepreneurship ecosystems programming.

The review aims to:

  1. Examine the relevance of existing entrepreneurship ecosystem interventions.
  2. Assess the effectiveness of programme implementation.
  3. Evaluate outcomes and impact achieved.
  4. Identify lessons learned from previous initiatives.
  5. Highlight best practices and success stories.
  6. Assess sustainability of interventions.
  7. Identify gaps and challenges.
  8. Provide recommendations for future programming.

The findings generated through the review will contribute to strengthening the ILO’s future entrepreneurship and MSME development strategies.

Key Areas Likely to be Examined

The review may focus on several dimensions of entrepreneurship ecosystem development, including:

1. Policy and Regulatory Environment

The expert may assess how ILO interventions have supported governments in creating favorable business environments through:

  • Entrepreneurship policies.
  • MSME support frameworks.
  • Regulatory reforms.
  • Business registration improvements.
  • Investment promotion initiatives.

2. Enterprise Development Services

The review may examine support provided to entrepreneurs through:

  • Business development services.
  • Mentorship programmes.
  • Entrepreneurship training.
  • Incubation and acceleration initiatives.
  • Skills development programmes.

3. Access to Finance

A critical area of entrepreneurship ecosystem development involves enabling entrepreneurs to access financial services.

The review may analyze interventions related to:

  • MSME financing.
  • Financial inclusion.
  • Investment readiness.
  • Business growth funding.
  • Access to credit.

4. Innovation and Digital Transformation

Modern entrepreneurship ecosystems increasingly rely on innovation and technology.

The review may evaluate programmes that support:

  • Digital entrepreneurship.
  • Technology adoption.
  • Innovation ecosystems.
  • Startup development.
  • Digital business models.

5. Inclusion and Equity

The ILO places strong emphasis on inclusive economic development.

The review may assess how programmes have supported:

  • Youth entrepreneurs.
  • Women entrepreneurs.
  • Rural enterprises.
  • Informal economy actors.
  • Vulnerable and marginalized groups.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This consultancy represents a significant opportunity for professionals working in entrepreneurship development and enterprise policy.

Key benefits include:

International Impact

The selected expert will contribute to shaping future entrepreneurship programming implemented across multiple countries and regions.

Collaboration with a UN Agency

Working with the ILO provides exposure to global development frameworks and international best practices.

Influence on Policy and Practice

Recommendations from the review could influence future entrepreneurship support programmes, MSME policies, and ecosystem development interventions.

Professional Recognition

Participation in a high-level international review demonstrates expertise in entrepreneurship development, programme evaluation, and economic development.

Desired Expertise

The ILO is expected to seek professionals with extensive experience in areas such as:

  • Entrepreneurship ecosystem development.
  • MSME development.
  • Enterprise promotion.
  • Economic development.
  • Programme evaluation.
  • Impact assessment.
  • Research and analysis.
  • Public policy.
  • Labour market development.
  • International development cooperation.

Candidates with experience working with governments, development agencies, international organizations, research institutions, or private-sector development programmes may be particularly competitive.

Expected Deliverables

Although detailed terms of reference may specify exact outputs, the selected expert may be expected to deliver:

  • Inception report.
  • Review framework and methodology.
  • Stakeholder consultations.
  • Data collection and analysis.
  • Draft review report.
  • Final review report.
  • Strategic recommendations.

Application Deadline

Applicants should consult the official UNGM notice for the exact submission deadline and application requirements. Interested experts are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible to avoid missing important deadlines.

Final Thoughts

The ILO Call for External Collaborator/Expert for the Review of Entrepreneurship Ecosystems Programming offers an outstanding opportunity for experienced enterprise development and MSME specialists to contribute to the future direction of entrepreneurship support initiatives worldwide.

By conducting a comprehensive review of the ILO’s entrepreneurship ecosystem programming, the selected expert will help strengthen interventions that support entrepreneurs, create jobs, foster innovation, and promote sustainable economic growth across diverse economies.

 

Discover more global job opportunites on OFY here

The post ILO Seeks External Expert to Review Global Entrepreneurship Ecosystems Programming: International Consultancy Opportunity for MSME and Enterprise Development Specialists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Food Programme (WFP) Digital Fundraising Internship 2026 in Rome, Italy: Receive Global Humanitarian Experience with the United Nations
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The United Nations World Food Programme (WFP), the world’s largest humanitarian organization and recipient of the 2020 Nobel Peace Prize, has announced applications for its Digital Fundraising Internship within the Private Partnerships and Fundraising (PPF) Division in Rome, Italy. This exciting international internship offers aspiring fundraising, communications, and digital marketing professionals the opportunity to gain […]

The post World Food Programme (WFP) Digital Fundraising Internship 2026 in Rome, Italy: Receive Global Humanitarian Experience with the United Nations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations World Food Programme (WFP), the world’s largest humanitarian organization and recipient of the 2020 Nobel Peace Prize, has announced applications for its Digital Fundraising Internship within the Private Partnerships and Fundraising (PPF) Division in Rome, Italy.

This exciting international internship offers aspiring fundraising, communications, and digital marketing professionals the opportunity to gain hands-on experience supporting one of the fastest-growing individual fundraising programs in the humanitarian sector. Successful candidates will work alongside experts managing global digital fundraising campaigns that reach hundreds of millions of people across more than 180 countries.

The internship is designed for individuals interested in humanitarian work, digital marketing, social media fundraising, campaign management, communications, and nonprofit fundraising strategy.

About the World Food Programme

The World Food Programme is the leading humanitarian organization fighting hunger worldwide. Every year, WFP provides life-saving food assistance and supports communities affected by conflict, disasters, economic crises, and climate-related emergencies.

In 2025 alone, WFP supported more than 100 million people around the world through emergency assistance, nutrition programs, and resilience-building initiatives.

To achieve its mission of creating a world with Zero Hunger, WFP has significantly expanded its private fundraising efforts. Its Individual Giving Programme has become one of the fastest-growing fundraising programs among major humanitarian organizations, with an ambitious goal of generating USD 1.4 billion in cumulative income between 2020 and 2030.

Internship Overview

  • Position: Digital Fundraising Intern
  • Organization: United Nations World Food Programme (WFP)
  • Division: Private Partnerships and Fundraising (PPF)
  • Duty Station: Rome, Italy
  • Contract Type: Internship
  • Duration: 8 Months
  • Expected Start Date: August 2026
  • Application Deadline: 24 June 2026 (23:59 GMT+01:00 Rome Time)

The selected intern will support the Individual Giving Global Supporter Acquisition Team and contribute to the technical implementation, coordination, optimization, and quality assurance of global fundraising campaigns.

Why Apply for the WFP Digital Fundraising Internship?

This internship provides a unique opportunity to work directly with a globally recognized United Nations agency while gaining valuable experience in digital fundraising and international communications.

Benefits include:

1. Work with a Nobel Peace Prize-Winning Organization

Interns will contribute to the fundraising operations of one of the most respected humanitarian organizations in the world.

2. Gain International Experience

Participants will work in an international and multicultural environment alongside professionals from diverse backgrounds and countries.

3. Develop Highly Marketable Skills

Interns will strengthen skills in:

  • Digital marketing
  • Social media advertising
  • Fundraising strategy
  • Campaign management
  • Content creation
  • Data analysis
  • Donor engagement
  • Humanitarian communications

4. Exposure to Global Digital Platforms

Interns will gain practical experience using platforms such as:

  • Meta Ads Manager
  • TikTok Ads Manager
  • Drupal
  • GoDonate
  • Tableau
  • Asana
  • SharePoint
  • Canva

5. Professional Development Opportunities

WFP invests significantly in employee development through:

  • Training programs
  • Mentorship opportunities
  • Coaching support
  • Professional networking
  • Internal mobility opportunities

Key Responsibilities

Successful candidates will support the fundraising team through a wide range of activities.

Digital Campaign Setup

Responsibilities include:

  • Supporting fundraising campaign setup on Meta and TikTok platforms
  • Managing donation page configurations through GoDonate
  • Ensuring accurate campaign naming and metadata management
  • Supporting URL tagging and redirect configuration
  • Assisting with campaign documentation and workflow management

Content and Creative Production

Interns will:

  • Help draft campaign clearance documents
  • Review photo and video content
  • Support development of social media creatives
  • Create draft graphics using Canva
  • Assist with copywriting and proofreading
  • Conduct research for celebrity fundraising initiatives

Campaign Analysis and Optimization

Interns may also:

  • Analyze fundraising campaign performance
  • Support troubleshooting of campaign issues
  • Review fundraising metrics using Tableau
  • Assist in preparing celebrity impact reports
  • Contribute to daily campaign optimization efforts

Expected Outcomes

By the end of the internship, the successful candidate will have helped ensure:

  • Smooth campaign launch processes
  • Accurate and efficient campaign setups
  • Effective fundraising content production
  • Strong operational support for acquisition campaigns
  • Improved campaign performance and reporting systems

Educational Requirements

Applicants should possess a university degree in one of the following areas:

  • Marketing
  • Communications
  • Business
  • International Relations
  • Or a related discipline

Desired Experience

Candidates should have some experience supporting teams in areas such as:

  • Digital marketing
  • Social media management
  • Fundraising
  • Creative production
  • Communications
  • Media operations

Preferred Technical Skills

Strong candidates will demonstrate:

  • Familiarity with Meta Ads Manager
  • Familiarity with TikTok Ads Manager
  • Knowledge of Drupal or similar web platforms
  • Experience with GoDonate or related tools
  • Canva design experience
  • Basic image editing skills
  • Microsoft Excel proficiency
  • Microsoft PowerPoint proficiency
  • Quality assurance and troubleshooting capabilities

Knowledge of digital fundraising is considered a strong advantage.

Required Soft Skills

WFP is seeking applicants who demonstrate:

  • Exceptional attention to detail
  • Strong organizational abilities
  • Collaboration and teamwork
  • Flexibility and adaptability
  • Ability to work in a hybrid environment
  • Strong communication skills
  • Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines

Language Requirements

Applicants must possess:

  • English proficiency at C2 level

Additional knowledge of the following languages is considered an advantage:

  • French
  • Arabic
  • German

Diversity and Inclusion

WFP strongly encourages applications from candidates regardless of:

  • Race
  • Ethnicity
  • Nationality
  • Gender
  • Disability status
  • Religion
  • Sexual orientation
  • Gender identity

The organization is committed to creating an inclusive and supportive work environment.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  1. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  2. Cover Letter

Additional documents may be requested during later stages of the recruitment process.

Apply Here 

Application Deadline

The deadline for submitting applications is:

24 June 2026 (23:59 GMT+01:00 Rome Time)

Interested candidates should submit their applications through the official WFP online recruitment platform.

Final Thoughts

The WFP Digital Fundraising Internship 2026 represents an exceptional opportunity for aspiring professionals seeking international experience in humanitarian fundraising, digital communications, and global campaign management.

Working with one of the world’s most influential humanitarian organizations will provide participants with valuable practical experience, professional growth opportunities, and exposure to cutting-edge digital fundraising strategies that support millions of vulnerable people around the world.

For more information on this internship opportunity visit here

For more internship opportunities on OFY click here  

The post World Food Programme (WFP) Digital Fundraising Internship 2026 in Rome, Italy: Receive Global Humanitarian Experience with the United Nations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UN Food Systems Youth Leadership Programme 2026–2028: Global Leadership Opportunity for Young Changemakers Transforming Food Systems Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Applications are now open for the second cohort of the United Nations Food Systems Youth Leadership Programme (YLP), a prestigious global initiative designed to empower young leaders who are committed to transforming food systems and advancing sustainable development around the world. Led by the United Nations Food Systems Coordination Hub and supported by the German […]

The post UN Food Systems Youth Leadership Programme 2026–2028: Global Leadership Opportunity for Young Changemakers Transforming Food Systems Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the second cohort of the United Nations Food Systems Youth Leadership Programme (YLP), a prestigious global initiative designed to empower young leaders who are committed to transforming food systems and advancing sustainable development around the world.

Led by the United Nations Food Systems Coordination Hub and supported by the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ), the Youth Leadership Programme brings together emerging leaders from diverse backgrounds to strengthen their leadership capacity, expand their networks, and accelerate meaningful action toward sustainable, inclusive, and resilient food systems.

Building on the success of its inaugural cohort, the programme is now expanding its global network of changemakers working across policy, entrepreneurship, science, advocacy, research, agriculture, climate action, and community development. Participants will gain access to a transformative leadership journey that equips them with practical skills, mentorship, peer learning opportunities, and exposure to global food systems processes.

For young leaders seeking to influence food systems transformation at local, national, regional, and global levels, this programme offers an exceptional opportunity to join a growing international community dedicated to creating lasting impact.

About the UN Food Systems Youth Leadership Programme

The UN Food Systems Youth Leadership Programme is more than a conventional training programme. It is a long-term leadership development initiative designed to nurture the next generation of leaders working at the intersection of food systems, sustainability, climate resilience, economic development, and social inclusion.

The programme recognizes that young people play a critical role in addressing some of the world’s most pressing challenges, including:

  • Food insecurity
  • Climate change
  • Sustainable agriculture
  • Nutrition and health
  • Environmental sustainability
  • Rural development
  • Social equity
  • Economic inclusion

Through structured learning, mentorship, collaboration, and leadership development, participants gain the tools and knowledge necessary to create meaningful change within their communities, institutions, organizations, and countries.

Why Food Systems Transformation Matters

Food systems touch every aspect of human life.

They influence:

  • Food production
  • Nutrition
  • Public health
  • Climate resilience
  • Biodiversity
  • Employment
  • Economic development
  • Social inclusion

However, food systems around the world face growing pressures, including:

  • Climate-related disruptions
  • Population growth
  • Resource scarcity
  • Economic inequalities
  • Environmental degradation
  • Food insecurity

Addressing these challenges requires innovative leadership, cross-sector collaboration, and systems thinking.

The Youth Leadership Programme aims to equip young leaders with the skills and perspectives needed to address these complex issues effectively.

Building on the Success of the First Cohort

The first cohort of the Youth Leadership Programme demonstrated the power of youth-led action and collaboration.

Over the past two years, participants have:

  • Strengthened food systems initiatives in their countries
  • Influenced policy discussions
  • Advanced research and innovation
  • Led advocacy campaigns
  • Built partnerships across sectors
  • Created collaborations that extend beyond borders

The impact of the programme continues to grow through the work of its alumni network.

One notable participant, Sofia Bacquerizo from Ecuador, highlighted the transformative nature of the programme by explaining how the relationships and knowledge gained through the YLP contributed directly to legislative reforms she is advancing in her country’s National Assembly.

Her experience illustrates how the programme helps participants turn ideas into tangible action and policy influence.

What Participants Will Gain

The Youth Leadership Programme offers a wide range of benefits designed to strengthen participants’ leadership capabilities and increase their impact.

1. Systems Thinking and Leadership Skills

Participants will develop a deeper understanding of food systems and learn how to navigate complex challenges through systems thinking approaches.

Key areas include:

  • Strategic leadership
  • Problem-solving
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Collaborative decision-making
  • Systems analysis

2. Access to a Global Network

Participants will become part of an international community of:

  • Youth leaders
  • Policymakers
  • Researchers
  • Entrepreneurs
  • Development practitioners
  • UN experts
  • Food systems specialists

This network creates opportunities for long-term collaboration and knowledge exchange.

3. Collaboration and Peer Learning

The programme emphasizes learning through collaboration.

Participants will engage with peers from different countries, cultures, and professional backgrounds, enabling them to:

  • Share experiences
  • Exchange ideas
  • Explore innovative solutions
  • Develop joint initiatives

4. Practical Leadership Tools

Participants will gain practical resources and methodologies that help strengthen their leadership effectiveness and project impact.

These tools can be applied directly to:

  • Community initiatives
  • Policy advocacy
  • Research projects
  • Entrepreneurship ventures
  • Development programmes

5. Exposure to Global Food Systems Processes

The programme provides valuable insight into international food systems governance and development processes.

Participants will gain exposure to:

  • United Nations initiatives
  • Global food systems dialogues
  • International partnerships
  • Policy frameworks
  • Sustainable development agendas

A Leadership Journey, Not Just a Training Programme

One of the most distinctive aspects of the Youth Leadership Programme is its long-term approach to leadership development.

Rather than focusing solely on technical training, the programme helps participants:

  • Build confidence
  • Expand influence
  • Strengthen leadership capacity
  • Develop strategic thinking skills
  • Foster meaningful relationships

This holistic approach supports participants in becoming effective leaders capable of driving change at multiple levels.

Who Should Apply?

The programme seeks young leaders who are actively contributing to food systems transformation and sustainable development.

Applications are particularly encouraged from:

Young Farmers

Individuals working to improve agricultural systems and food production practices.

Entrepreneurs

Founders and innovators creating solutions for food systems challenges.

Policy Advocates

Young professionals engaged in policy development, advocacy, and governance.

Researchers

Individuals conducting research related to food systems, sustainability, climate, agriculture, nutrition, or development.

Science Communicators

Professionals who translate scientific knowledge into accessible information for broader audiences.

Indigenous and Local Community Representatives

Leaders working to preserve and promote community knowledge and sustainable practices.

Civil Society Leaders

Individuals leading initiatives that contribute to social, environmental, and economic development.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet all of the following requirements:

Age Requirement

Applicants must be between 18 and 35 years old.

Language Requirement

Applicants must be proficient in English.

Nationality Requirement

Applicants must be nationals of low- or middle-income countries.

Priority regions include:

  • Africa
  • Asia and the Pacific
  • Latin America and the Caribbean

Professional Experience Requirement

Applicants should demonstrate experience in:

  • Food systems
  • Sustainable development
  • Climate action
  • Related fields

Leadership Experience

Applicants must have experience leading or contributing to initiatives within:

  • Communities
  • Organizations
  • Institutions
  • Sectors

Programme Commitment

Applicants must be willing to participate fully in programme activities through 2028.

Why You Should Apply

The Youth Leadership Programme is ideal for young changemakers seeking to:

  • Strengthen leadership skills
  • Expand professional networks
  • Increase policy influence
  • Collaborate internationally
  • Advance food systems transformation
  • Contribute to sustainable development goals
  • Build long-term partnerships
  • Access mentorship opportunities

The programme provides a unique platform to amplify youth voices in global conversations and decision-making processes.

Important Deadline

Applications Close:

13 July 2026

Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline to maximize their chances of consideration.

APPLY HERE

How This Programme Can Shape Your Future

Participation in the Youth Leadership Programme can open doors to:

  • International collaborations
  • Leadership opportunities
  • Policy engagement
  • Professional growth
  • Research partnerships
  • Advocacy initiatives
  • Global development networks

Participants join a growing community of leaders working across sectors to address complex food systems challenges and create sustainable solutions.

Click HERE to learn more about UN Food Systems Youth Leadership Programme 2026–2028.

Conclusion

The UN Food Systems Youth Leadership Programme 2026–2028 represents an outstanding opportunity for young leaders from low- and middle-income countries to develop the skills, networks, and knowledge needed to drive transformative change in food systems and sustainable development. Through mentorship, systems thinking, peer learning, and global collaboration, participants will gain valuable experience that strengthens their ability to influence policies, lead initiatives, and create lasting impact within their communities and beyond.

For young farmers, entrepreneurs, researchers, advocates, climate leaders, and development practitioners who are passionate about creating more sustainable, equitable, and resilient food systems, this programme offers an unparalleled platform for leadership growth and global engagement. Interested candidates should submit their applications before 13 July 2026 and take the next step toward becoming part of a powerful international network of changemakers.

Discover more global Fellowship, Leadership Development, and UN opportunities on Opportunities for Youths (OFY).

The post UN Food Systems Youth Leadership Programme 2026–2028: Global Leadership Opportunity for Young Changemakers Transforming Food Systems Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Unlock the Power of Intellectual Property: Your Complete Guide to 16 Free WIPO Online Courses (2026–2027)
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
In today‘s innovation-driven global economy, intellectual property has become one of the most valuable assets any individual, business, or nation can possess. Whether you are an entrepreneur protecting a groundbreaking invention, a creative professional safeguarding your artistic works, a legal practitioner advising clients on IP strategy, or a policymaker shaping the innovation landscape, understanding intellectual […]

The post Unlock the Power of Intellectual Property: Your Complete Guide to 16 Free WIPO Online Courses (2026–2027) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
In today‘s innovation-driven global economy, intellectual property has become one of the most valuable assets any individual, business, or nation can possess. Whether you are an entrepreneur protecting a groundbreaking invention, a creative professional safeguarding your artistic works, a legal practitioner advising clients on IP strategy, or a policymaker shaping the innovation landscape, understanding intellectual property is no longer optional—it is essential.

The World Intellectual Property Organization, through its WIPO Academy, offers a comprehensive suite of free online courses designed to take learners from absolute beginner to advanced specialist. With 16 courses covering everything from copyright and patents to trademarks, traditional knowledge, arbitration, software licensing, and export strategy, this is one of the most valuable free educational resources available anywhere in the world.

This guide provides a complete overview of every course in the WIPO Academy portfolio available on the UN SDG:Learn platform. Whether you are just starting your IP journey or looking to deepen your expertise in a specific area, you will find detailed information about each course, including level, duration, language availability, registration deadlines, and learning objectives.

All courses are offered entirely free of charge by the World Intellectual Property Organization, a United Nations partner agency, and are available in multiple languages including English, French, Spanish, Arabic, Russian, and Portuguese.

Course Portfolio Overview

The WIPO Academy course体系 follows a progressive learning pathway. Beginners start with foundational courses that build core knowledge, then advance to specialized courses tailored to specific professional interests and career goals. The two introductory courses—DL-001 and DL-101—are strongly recommended as prerequisites for all advanced courses.

 

PART ONE: FOUNDATIONAL COURSES

  1. DL-001: Primer on Intellectual Property

Course Overview:
This self-paced introductory course provides a comprehensive overview of the fundamental aspects of intellectual property, covering copyright and related rights, trademarks, patents, geographical indications, and industrial designs. It also explores current topics impacting IP, including plant variety rights, unfair competition, the enforcement of IP rights, and emerging issues in the field.

Course Structure:
The course consists of 12 sequential modules designed to help participants progressively build their understanding of IP concepts. Self-assessment tools are woven throughout the modules to help learners test their knowledge and ability to apply concepts and key facts.

Assessment and Certification:
There is no final examination. Participants move through the modules based on self-assessed progress and readiness. Upon completion, learners are encouraged to move on to DL-101.

Key Details:

  • Level: Introductory
  • Duration: 3 hours
  • Effort: 3 hours
  • Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish, Arabic, Portuguese
  • Price: Free
  • Certificate: No
  • Start Date: 30 January 2027
  • End Date: 15 January 2027
  • Registration Deadline: 12 October 2027

Target Audience:
This course is designed for anyone seeking a general overview of IP fundamentals—no prior expertise in the field is required.

How to Access:
Click “Visit course page” at the top of the course page. Create a WIPO Academy account, set up your profile, and register on the WIPO Academy platform.

 

  1. DL-101: General Course on Intellectual Property

Course Overview:
Building on the concepts introduced in DL-001, this course provides a more comprehensive presentation of the main areas of intellectual property. It covers copyright, related rights, patents, trademarks, geographical indications, industrial designs, plant variety protection, unfair competition, and the treaties administered by WIPO.

Course Structure:
The General Course on Intellectual Property consists of 13 self-paced modules. Self-assessment tools are strategically placed at the end of each module to help participants gauge their understanding, monitor their progress, and evaluate their ability to apply the concepts and information covered.

Assessment and Certification:
A time-bound final examination, delivered online and composed of multiple-choice questions, awards successful participants an electronic certificate of completion.

Key Details:

  • Level: Introductory
  • Duration: 6 weeks
  • Effort: 55 hours
  • Languages: English, French
  • Price: Free
  • Certificate: Yes
  • Start Date: 30 January 2026
  • End Date: 15 December 2026
  • Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026

Target Audience:
This course is designed for beginners, students, and professionals seeking a solid, self-guided introduction to the IP system. It is suitable for:

  • Students, Researchers, and Academics
  • Entrepreneurs, Startup Founders, and Innovators
  • Legal Professionals and Paralegals
  • Government Officials and Policymakers
  • Business Professionals and Managers
  • Artists, Designers, and Creators
  • General Public and Lifelong Learners

Learning Objectives:
By the end of the course, participants will be able to:

  • Understand the fundamental concepts of intellectual property
  • Recognize the role of IP in society and the economy
  • Identify the main types of IP rights and their functions
  • Describe the purpose, scope, and duration of key IP rights
  • Understand the international framework for IP protection
  • Analyze the application of IP in different contexts
  • Interpret the relationship between IP and development
  • Apply basic IP knowledge practically

How to Access:
Click “Visit course page” at the top of the course page. Create a WIPO Academy account, set up your profile, and register on the WIPO Academy platform.

 

PART TWO: ADVANCED SPECIALIZED COURSES

The following advanced courses are designed for learners who have completed DL-101 and wish to deepen their expertise in specific areas of intellectual property. All advanced courses are free, self-paced, and do not offer certificates unless otherwise noted.

 

  1. DL-201: Copyright and Related Rights

Course Overview:
This advanced course covers the fundamental principles of international copyright and related rights protection, providing a comprehensive overview of international treaties, recent developments, and trends in the area of international copyright. It describes the system of collective management of rights and addresses issues of copyright and related rights in the digital environment. The course also covers the role of WIPO in the worldwide protection of copyright.

Prerequisites:
Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended.

Course Structure:
The course is composed of 13 modules, each containing specific learning objectives.

Key Details:

  • Level: Advanced
  • Effort: 100 hours total
  • Languages: English, French
  • Price: Free
  • Certificate: No
  • Start Date: 30 January 2026
  • End Date: 12 March 2027
  • Registration Deadline: 12 October 2026

Target Audience:
This course is designed for individuals seeking an advanced understanding of international copyright and related rights, including:

  • Legal professionals
  • Government officials and policymakers
  • Academics, researchers, and advanced students
  • Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations
  • Professionals in the creative industries
  • WIPO personnel and IP office staff

Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course.

 

  1. DL-203: Intellectual Property, Traditional Knowledge and Traditional Cultural Expressions

Course Overview:
This self-paced advanced course introduces key concepts and policy issues in the protection of traditional knowledge and traditional cultural expressions through intellectual property. It provides technical information on key concepts, international, regional, and national experiences, policy options, and legal mechanisms available or under consideration for the IP protection of traditional knowledge and traditional cultural expressions.

Prerequisites:
Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended.

Key Details:

  • Level: Advanced
  • Effort: 100 hours total
  • Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish
  • Price: Free
  • Certificate: No
  • Start Date: 30 January 2026
  • End Date: 12 March 2027
  • Registration Deadline: 12 October 2026

Target Audience:
This course is particularly suited for:

  • Legal professionals
  • Government officials and policymakers
  • Academics, researchers, and advanced students
  • Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations
  • Professionals in the creative industries
  • WIPO personnel and IP office staff

Learning Objectives:
Learners will examine legal mechanisms currently available or under discussion, including those under negotiation at WIPO.

Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course.

 

  1. DL-301: Patents

Course Overview:
DL-301 provides participants with an in-depth perspective on the principles and economic impact of patents and the patenting process as a whole. The course examines the traditional use of patents by centers of knowledge such as universities and other specialized institutions of higher learning. It also provides a comprehensive review of the importance of patent pools in facilitating the effective transfer and development of technology.

Prerequisites:
Completion of DL-101 is highly recommended.

Key Details:

  • Level: Advanced
  • Length: 100 hours
  • Effort: Self-paced
  • Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish
  • Price: Free
  • Certificate: No
  • Start Date: 30 January 2026
  • End Date: 15 December 2026
  • Registration Deadline: 12 October 2026

Target Audience:
DL-301 is designed for individuals seeking an advanced understanding of the patent system, innovation management, technology transfer, or related fields, including:

  • Legal professionals
  • Government officials and policymakers
  • Academics, researchers, and advanced students
  • Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations
  • Professionals in the creative industries
  • WIPO personnel and IP office staff

Learning Objectives:
Participants will explore the legal framework of patents, the requirements for patentability, and the strategic role patents play in innovation and economic development.

Note: There are no tutorials or exams for this course, and no certificate is offered.

 

  1. DL-302: Trademarks, Industrial Designs and Geographical Indications

Course Overview:
DL-302 covers intellectual property aspects closely related to trade and competition in the globalized market. It educates and raises awareness of complex issues surrounding the protection and management of intellectual property for branding. The course serves as a preparatory course designed to precede practical training in the protection of trademarks, industrial designs, and geographical indications.

Prerequisites:
Completion of DL-101 is recommended.

Key Details:

  • Level: Advanced
  • Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish
  • Price: Free
  • Certificate: No
  • Start Date: 30 January 2026
  • End Date: 15 December 2026
  • Registration Deadline: 12 October 2026

Target Audience:
This course is designed for:

  • Legal professionals
  • Government officials and policymakers
  • Academics, researchers, and advanced students
  • Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations
  • Professionals in the creative industries
  • WIPO personnel and IP office staff

Learning Objectives:
Participants will be able to:

  • Explain the legal frameworks and key concepts governing the protection of trademarks, industrial designs, and geographical indications
  • Compare and contrast different national and regional approaches to trademarks, designs, and geographical indications
  • Apply advanced knowledge to case studies involving complex IP disputes or policy challenges

Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course.

 

  1. DL-317: Arbitration and Mediation Procedure under the WIPO Rules

Course Overview:
This advanced online course provides a comprehensive overview of the principal features and advantages of the procedures administered by the WIPO Arbitration and Mediation Center. It describes the main features and advantages of the procedures designed by the Center, the selection process of the most appropriate dispute resolution procedure for any given case, and how to draft appropriate dispute resolution clauses for insertion into an agreement.

Prerequisites:
Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended.

Key Details:

  • Level: Advanced
  • Length: 100 hours total
  • Effort: Self-paced
  • Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish
  • Price: Free
  • Certificate: No
  • Start Date: 1 June 2028
  • End Date: 12 March 2027
  • Registration Deadline: 12 October 2026

Target Audience:
This course is particularly suited for:

  • Legal professionals
  • Government officials and policymakers
  • Academics, researchers, and advanced students
  • Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations
  • Professionals in the creative industries
  • WIPO personnel and IP office staff

Learning Objectives:
Participants will:

  • Understand the main features and advantages of the procedures designed by the WIPO Arbitration and Mediation Center
  • Analyze different case scenarios to evaluate and select the most appropriate dispute resolution procedure
  • Apply drafting principles to create appropriate dispute resolution clauses for inclusion in agreements

Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course.

 

  1. DL-318: Patent Information Search

Course Overview:
This course is designed to introduce and illustrate key concepts in patent searching. Practical activities have been incorporated throughout the course to complement theoretical principles that underpin patent information searching. Participants are furnished with in-depth information regarding sources, techniques, and data used during patent searches.

Prerequisites:
Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended.

Key Details:

  • Level: Advanced
  • Length: 100 hours
  • Effort: Self-paced
  • Languages: English, French, Spanish
  • Price: Free
  • Certificate: No
  • Start Date: 30 January 2026
  • End Date: 15 December 2026
  • Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026

Target Audience:
The course is particularly suited for:

  • Legal professionals
  • Government officials and policymakers
  • Academics, researchers, and advanced students
  • Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations
  • Professionals in the creative industries
  • WIPO personnel and IP office staff

Learning Objectives:
Participants will gain in-depth knowledge of the sources, techniques, and data used in conducting patent searches.

Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course.

 

  1. DL-320: Basics of Patent Drafting

Course Overview:
This advanced distance learning course introduces and illustrates the concepts of patent drafting. It is neither a qualification course for patent drafting nor a replacement of existing training manuals for patent attorneys or patent agents in countries with robust professional education systems. Some practical exercises are proposed with enough background and description to help learners gain confidence to form a credible set of claims. The course material has been designed on the basis of common denominators of several national patent laws in which most applicants seek patent protection.

Prerequisites:
Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended.

Key Details:

  • Level: Advanced
  • Length: Self-paced
  • Effort: 100 hours
  • Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish, Arabic, Portuguese
  • Price: Free
  • Certificate: No
  • Start Date: 30 January 2026
  • End Date: 15 December 2026
  • Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026

Target Audience:

  • Legal professionals
  • Government officials and policymakers
  • Academics, researchers, and advanced students
  • Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations
  • Professionals in the creative industries
  • WIPO personnel and IP office staff

Learning Objectives:
Participants will be able to:

  • Understand the fundamental concepts and principles of patent drafting
  • Identify the key elements that make up a patent claim
  • Apply drafting techniques through guided explanations and practical exercises
  • Develop a structured and credible set of patent claims with increasing confidence

Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course.

 

  1. DL-450: Intellectual Property Management

Course Overview:
This course examines the economic and strategic dimensions of intellectual property. It focuses on IP from the perspective of “why” and “how” for participants who have already covered the basics of “what” IP is. The first three modules teach about the economic significance of IP, covering IP asset identification, IP incubation, IP commercialization, IP valuation, and IP taxation. The last three modules offer an in-depth look at commercial activities in the digital area, including e-commerce and IP, digital management of creative works, and the strategic management of IP.

Prerequisites:
Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended.

Key Details:

  • Level: Advanced
  • Length: Self-paced
  • Effort: 100 hours
  • Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish, Arabic, Portuguese
  • Price: Free
  • Certificate: No
  • Start Date: 30 January 2026
  • End Date: 15 December 2026
  • Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026

Target Audience:

  • Legal professionals
  • Government officials and policymakers
  • Academics, researchers, and advanced students
  • Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations
  • Professionals in the creative industries
  • WIPO personnel and IP office staff

Learning Objectives:
Participants will be able to:

  • Understand the economic significance of intellectual property and its role in business management
  • Apply best practices for identifying, valuing, and commercializing IP assets
  • Develop strategies for managing IP in digital environments and leveraging it for commercial advantage

Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course.

 

PART THREE: COLLECTIVE MANAGEMENT OF COPYRIGHT AND RELATED RIGHTS – A Five-Course Series

The following five courses examine collective management of copyright and related rights from the perspective of different stakeholders. Together, they form a comprehensive learning pathway for anyone seeking expertise in this specialized area.

 

  1. DL-501: Collective Management of Copyright and Related Rights for Legal Practitioners

Course Overview:
This course introduces legal practitioners to collective management of copyright, covering CMO operations, agreements, tariff disputes, and international treaties, while highlighting the benefits of collective management for creators, users, and society. Legal practitioners need to understand what CMOs do, how they acquire mandates from rights holders, and how they license the use of protected works.

Prerequisites:
Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended.

Key Details:

  • Level: Advanced
  • Length: Self-paced
  • Effort: 100 hours
  • Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish, Arabic, Portuguese
  • Price: Free
  • Certificate: No
  • Start Date: 30 January 2026
  • End Date: 15 December 2026
  • Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026

Target Audience:

  • Legal professionals
  • Government officials and policymakers
  • Academics, researchers, and advanced students
  • Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations
  • Professionals in the creative industries
  • WIPO personnel and IP office staff

Learning Objectives:
Participants will understand the role and functions of collective management organizations and their legal framework, and apply knowledge of international copyright treaties to practical legal scenarios in collective management.

Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course.

 

  1. DL-502: Collective Management of Copyright and Related Rights for Rights Holders

Course Overview:
This course explores collective management of copyright, showing how CMOs operate across music, text, visual arts, and dramatic works, and highlighting benefits for creators, users, legislators, and society. It is targeted at anyone intending to learn in-depth about collective management of copyright, with a particular focus on primary rights holders.

Prerequisites:
Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended.

Key Details:

  • Level: Advanced
  • Length: Self-paced
  • Effort: 100 hours
  • Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish, Arabic, Portuguese
  • Price: Free
  • Certificate: No
  • Start Date: 30 January 2026
  • End Date: 15 December 2026
  • Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026

Target Audience:

  • Legal professionals
  • Government officials and policymakers
  • Academics, researchers, and advanced students
  • Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations
  • Professionals in the creative industries
  • WIPO personnel and IP office staff

Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course.

 

  1. DL-503: Collective Management of Copyright and Related Rights for Collective Management Organizations (CMOs)

Course Overview:
This course is intended for anyone wishing to gain an in-depth understanding of collective management, in particular collective management organizations. It covers the workings of collective management of copyright in the categories of music, text and images, works of visual art and photography, and dramatic works. The course also discusses the day-to-day work of CMOs and their governance, and examines the challenges and opportunities brought about by digital technologies.

Prerequisites:
Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended.

Key Details:

  • Level: Advanced
  • Length: Self-paced
  • Effort: 100 hours
  • Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish, Arabic, Portuguese
  • Price: Free
  • Certificate: No
  • Start Date: 30 January 2026
  • End Date: 15 December 2026
  • Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026

Target Audience:

  • Legal professionals
  • Government officials and policymakers
  • Academics, researchers, and advanced students
  • Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations
  • Professionals in the creative industries
  • WIPO personnel and IP office staff

Learning Objectives:
Participants will understand:

  • The role and benefits of collective management for creators, users, legislators, and society
  • How CMOs operate across different categories of copyright works
  • The interaction of legislation, policy, and rights management in an effective copyright system
  • The challenges and opportunities in collective management brought by digital technologies

Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course.

 

  1. DL-506: Collective Management of Copyright and Related Rights for Policy Makers

Course Overview:
This course introduces policy-making in copyright and collective management, exploring legislative frameworks, enforcement, and the role of government in supporting creators, users, and society across various creative sectors. Policy makers have a direct role in legislation and enforcement, with the primary role of laying down legislative frameworks for copyright and collective management.

Prerequisites:
Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended.

Key Details:

  • Level: Advanced
  • Length: Self-paced
  • Effort: 100 hours
  • Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish, Arabic, Portuguese
  • Price: Free
  • Certificate: No
  • Start Date: 30 January 2026
  • End Date: 15 December 2026
  • Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026

Target Audience:

  • Legal professionals
  • Government officials and policymakers
  • Academics, researchers, and advanced students
