  • Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations
  • Professionals in the creative industries
  • WIPO personnel and IP office staff

Learning Objectives:
Participants will:

  • Understand the role of policy-makers in copyright legislation, enforcement, and collective management
  • Compare different legislative frameworks for collective management and their impact on stakeholders
  • Assess how government involvement in collective management benefits creators, users, and society

Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course.

 

PART FOUR: OTHER ADVANCED SPECIALIZED COURSES

  1. DL-511: Software Licensing Including Open Source

Course Overview:
This course explores proprietary and open source software, their implications, and licensing. The objective is to enhance awareness of the possibilities and implications of proprietary and open source software, and to create useful sources of information including concrete examples and bibliography on software licensing, with special emphasis on the needs of developing countries.

Prerequisites:
Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended.

Key Details:

  • Level: Advanced
  • Length: Self-paced
  • Effort: 100 hours
  • Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish, Arabic, Portuguese
  • Price: Free
  • Certificate: No
  • Start Date: 30 January 2026
  • End Date: 15 December 2026
  • Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026

Target Audience:

  • Legal professionals
  • Government officials and policymakers
  • Academics, researchers, and advanced students
  • Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations
  • Professionals in the creative industries
  • WIPO personnel and IP office staff

Learning Objectives:
Participants will:

  • Understand the key differences and implications of proprietary and open source software
  • Apply their learning to identify relevant licensing frameworks and their practical applications
  • Create useful resources, including examples and bibliographies, to support software licensing decisions, with emphasis on developing country contexts

Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course.

 

  1. DL-730: Executive Course on Intellectual Property and Exports

Course Overview:
DL-730 explores how intellectual property supports the protection and commercialization of goods and services in exports, providing practical skills for exporters and export agency professionals. Intellectual property is a key tool that facilitates both the protection and commercialization of goods and services at national, regional, and international levels. The course uses a practical, skills-focused methodology to address the common challenges and issues faced by exporters and export agency workers in everyday practice.

Prerequisites:
Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended.

Key Details:

  • Level: Advanced
  • Length: Self-paced
  • Effort: 100 hours
  • Languages: English, Spanish, Portuguese
  • Price: Free
  • Certificate: No
  • Start Date: 30 January 2026
  • End Date: 15 December 2026
  • Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026

Target Audience:

  • Legal professionals
  • Government officials and policymakers
  • Academics, researchers, and advanced students
  • Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations
  • Professionals in the creative industries
  • WIPO personnel and IP office staff

Learning Objectives:
Participants will:

  • Understand the role of intellectual property in the protection and commercialization of goods and services for export
  • Learn to identify common IP-related challenges faced by exporters and export agencies
  • Apply practical strategies to leverage IP effectively in international trade and market expansion

Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course.

 

How to Register for Any Course

All courses are accessible through the UN SDG:Learn platform. The registration process is identical for all courses:

  1. Navigate to the course page on UN SDG:Learn
  2. Click the “Visit course page” button at the top of the page
  3. Create a WIPO Academy account (if you do not already have one)
  4. Set up your profile
  5. Register for the course on the WIPO Academy platform

 

Summary of Key Dates

Course Start Date End Date Registration Deadline
DL-001 Primer on IP 30 Jan 2027 15 Jan 2027 12 Oct 2027
DL-101 General Course on IP 30 Jan 2026 15 Dec 2026 10 Dec 2026
DL-201 Copyright and Related Rights 30 Jan 2026 12 Mar 2027 12 Oct 2026
DL-203 IP, Traditional Knowledge & Cultural Expressions 30 Jan 2026 12 Mar 2027 12 Oct 2026
DL-301 Patents 30 Jan 2026 15 Dec 2026 12 Oct 2026
DL-302 Trademarks, Industrial Designs & GI 30 Jan 2026 15 Dec 2026 12 Oct 2026
DL-317 Arbitration and Mediation 1 Jun 2028 12 Mar 2027 12 Oct 2026
DL-318 Patent Information Search 30 Jan 2026 15 Dec 2026 10 Dec 2026
DL-320 Basics of Patent Drafting 30 Jan 2026 15 Dec 2026 10 Dec 2026
DL-450 IP Management 30 Jan 2026 15 Dec 2026 10 Dec 2026
DL-501 Collective Management for Legal Practitioners 30 Jan 2026 15 Dec 2026 10 Dec 2026
DL-502 Collective Management for Rights Holders 30 Jan 2026 15 Dec 2026 10 Dec 2026
DL-503 Collective Management for CMOs 30 Jan 2026 15 Dec 2026 10 Dec 2026
DL-506 Collective Management for Policy Makers 30 Jan 2026 15 Dec 2026 10 Dec 2026
DL-511 Software Licensing Including Open Source 30 Jan 2026 15 Dec 2026 10 Dec 2026
DL-730 IP and Exports 30 Jan 2026 15 Dec 2026 10 Dec 2026

 

Recommended Learning Pathways

Pathway 1: General IP Knowledge
DL-001 → DL-101 → Any advanced course based on interest

Pathway 2: Copyright Specialization
DL-001 → DL-101 → DL-201 → DL-501 / DL-502 / DL-503 / DL-506 (Collective Management series)

Pathway 3: Patent Specialization
DL-001 → DL-101 → DL-301 → DL-318 / DL-320

Pathway 4: Business and Commercialization
DL-001 → DL-101 → DL-450 → DL-730

Pathway 5: Dispute Resolution
DL-001 → DL-101 → DL-317

Pathway 6: Traditional Knowledge and Cultural Expressions
DL-001 → DL-101 → DL-203

Pathway 7: Technology and Software
DL-001 → DL-101 → DL-511

 

Conclusion

The World Intellectual Property Organization, through its WIPO Academy and in partnership with UN SDG:Learn, has created one of the most comprehensive and accessible intellectual property education programs available anywhere in the world. With 16 free courses spanning introductory through advanced levels, learners can build expertise in copyright, patents, trademarks, industrial designs, geographical indications, traditional knowledge, arbitration, patent drafting, IP management, collective management, software licensing, and export strategy.

All courses are completely free, self-paced, and available in multiple languages. Whether you are a student, professional, entrepreneur, policymaker, or lifelong learner, these courses offer an unparalleled opportunity to develop valuable IP knowledge and skills that can advance your career and contribute to innovation and economic development.

Do not delay—registration deadlines vary by course, with the earliest deadlines in October and December 2026. Create your WIPO Academy account today and begin your intellectual property learning journey.

 

Learn More

  • More global short courses opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Unlock the Power of Intellectual Property: Your Complete Guide to 16 Free WIPO Online Courses (2026–2027) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Harvard Radcliffe Institute Fellowship Program 2027–2028: Prestigious Fully Funded Fellowship at Harvard University for Scholars, Scientists, Writers, Journalists, Artists, and Public Intellectuals
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Harvard Radcliffe Institute Fellowship Program 2027–2028 is now accepting applications from exceptional scholars, scientists, researchers, writers, journalists, artists, and public intellectuals from around the world. Hosted by the Harvard Radcliffe Institute for Advanced Study at Harvard University, this highly competitive fellowship offers participants a rare and transformative opportunity to spend a full academic year […]

The post Harvard Radcliffe Institute Fellowship Program 2027–2028: Prestigious Fully Funded Fellowship at Harvard University for Scholars, Scientists, Writers, Journalists, Artists, and Public Intellectuals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Harvard Radcliffe Institute Fellowship Program 2027–2028 is now accepting applications from exceptional scholars, scientists, researchers, writers, journalists, artists, and public intellectuals from around the world. Hosted by the Harvard Radcliffe Institute for Advanced Study at Harvard University, this highly competitive fellowship offers participants a rare and transformative opportunity to spend a full academic year pursuing ambitious projects within one of the world’s most intellectually vibrant academic environments.

Widely recognized as one of the most prestigious interdisciplinary fellowship programs globally, the Harvard Radcliffe Institute Fellowship provides fellows with access to Harvard University’s unparalleled resources, world-class faculty, renowned libraries, research facilities, and a dynamic community of thinkers and innovators.

The fellowship is specifically designed for individuals who are pursuing innovative work that addresses important social, scientific, artistic, and policy challenges while engaging audiences beyond traditional academic circles.

For those seeking time, resources, intellectual freedom, and interdisciplinary collaboration to advance a significant project, the Harvard Radcliffe Institute Fellowship Program represents an extraordinary opportunity.

About the Harvard Radcliffe Institute Fellowship Program

The Harvard Radcliffe Institute Fellowship Program brings together exceptional individuals from diverse disciplines and professional backgrounds to engage in advanced research, creative work, scholarly inquiry, and intellectual exploration.

Unlike many traditional fellowships that focus on a single discipline or area of study, the Radcliffe Fellowship is intentionally interdisciplinary. It creates an environment where scientists collaborate with artists, historians exchange ideas with engineers, journalists interact with policy experts, and scholars engage with public intellectuals.

This unique model encourages intellectual cross-pollination and helps generate innovative solutions to complex global challenges.

The fellowship provides participants with the opportunity to:

  • Focus deeply on a major project
  • Collaborate across disciplines
  • Engage with Harvard’s academic community
  • Access extensive research resources
  • Build long-lasting professional networks
  • Advance public scholarship and impact

Why the Harvard Radcliffe Fellowship Is Unique

The Harvard Radcliffe Institute has built a reputation for supporting transformative work that expands knowledge, challenges conventional thinking, and contributes meaningfully to society.

The fellowship is not simply a funding opportunity. It is an immersive intellectual experience that gives participants the time and freedom to pursue ambitious ideas that might otherwise be difficult to undertake.

Key distinguishing features include:

Interdisciplinary Community

Fellows join a diverse cohort that includes:

  • Scientists
  • Historians
  • Economists
  • Journalists
  • Writers
  • Artists
  • Policy experts
  • Humanists
  • Social scientists

This multidisciplinary environment creates opportunities for collaboration and innovation that rarely exist within traditional academic structures.

Harvard University Resources

Fellows gain access to:

  • Harvard libraries
  • Research centers
  • Academic departments
  • Faculty expertise
  • Harvard events and seminars
  • Specialized collections and archives

Intellectual Freedom

Participants are encouraged to explore bold ideas and innovative approaches that can shape future scholarship, public discourse, and policy.

Areas of Interest and Preferred Project Themes

The Institute welcomes proposals addressing a broad range of disciplines and issues.

Particular interest is given to projects that:

  • Address pressing social challenges
  • Explore major scientific questions
  • Advance public policy discussions
  • Engage audiences beyond academia
  • Contribute meaningful intellectual and societal impact

Women, Gender, and Society Research

Reflecting the historical legacy of Radcliffe Institute and the Schlesinger Library, applicants are encouraged to submit projects that explore:

  • Women’s history
  • Gender studies
  • Feminist scholarship
  • Society and social change
  • Gender equity and inclusion

However, applicants are not required to focus on these themes to be eligible.

Special Focus Area: Academic Freedom and Connecting Across Difference (2024–2029)

One of the Institute’s major thematic priorities for 2024–2029 is Academic Freedom and Connecting Across Difference.

Applicants are encouraged to develop projects related to:

Academic Freedom

Research examining:

  • Free inquiry
  • Intellectual diversity
  • Freedom of expression
  • Higher education policy

Diversity of Thought

Projects exploring:

  • Constructive disagreement
  • Intellectual pluralism
  • Democratic discourse
  • Dialogue across differences

Political Polarization

Research investigating:

  • Social cohesion
  • Political divisions
  • Civic engagement
  • Conflict resolution

Peace and Conflict

Studies addressing:

  • Peacebuilding
  • International relations
  • Reconciliation
  • Conflict transformation

Inequality and Social Justice

Projects focused on:

  • Economic inequality
  • Educational equity
  • Social mobility
  • Public policy solutions

Religious Pluralism

Research examining:

  • Religion and public life
  • Religion and family life
  • Interfaith dialogue
  • Religious diversity

The Institute also welcomes proposals that challenge conventional assumptions and introduce innovative perspectives capable of reshaping existing scholarly conversations.

Fellowship Disciplines

Applications are accepted in multiple categories.

Humanities and Social Sciences

Examples include:

  • History
  • Sociology
  • Anthropology
  • Political Science
  • Economics
  • Philosophy
  • Literature

Creative Arts

Including:

  • Visual arts
  • Performing arts
  • Creative writing
  • Film
  • Design

Nonfiction and Journalism

Including:

  • Investigative journalism
  • Narrative nonfiction
  • Public scholarship
  • Media projects

Science, Engineering, and Mathematics

Including:

  • Biology
  • Physics
  • Chemistry
  • Computer Science
  • Engineering
  • Mathematics
  • Environmental Science

Benefits of the Harvard Radcliffe Fellowship

The fellowship offers exceptional professional and intellectual benefits.

Dedicated Time for Research and Creative Work

Participants can devote a full academic year to advancing a significant project.

Access to Harvard University

Fellows benefit from one of the world’s most prestigious academic environments.

Interdisciplinary Collaboration

Opportunities to interact with experts from numerous fields.

Professional Visibility

Participation enhances professional recognition and expands scholarly influence.

Intellectual Community

Fellows become part of an influential global network of researchers, writers, artists, and innovators.

Access to Schlesinger Library Collections

Applicants whose work aligns with the library’s collections may gain access to unique historical resources.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must satisfy discipline-specific eligibility requirements.

Key requirements include:

Eligible Applicants

  • Scholars
  • Scientists
  • Researchers
  • Writers
  • Journalists
  • Artists
  • Public intellectuals

Career Stage

Applications are welcomed from:

  • Early-career professionals
  • Mid-career experts
  • Senior scholars and practitioners

Tenure Status

Academic applicants do not need tenure to apply.

Degree Enrollment Restriction

Individuals currently enrolled in a degree program are not eligible.

Previous Fellows Restriction

Former Harvard Radcliffe Fellows (1999 to present) are not eligible to apply.

Application Materials

Applicants must submit a complete application package through the online application portal.

Required materials include:

1. Online Application Form

Applicants must complete all required sections.

2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

A current professional CV outlining achievements and experience.

3. Project Proposal

Applicants must submit a project proposal of up to 1,400 words.

The proposal should clearly explain:

  • Research or creative objectives
  • Methodology
  • Expected outcomes
  • Potential impact

4. Bibliography

Required where appropriate.

5. Writing or Work Sample

Applicants must provide a sample demonstrating the quality of their work.

6. Three References

Applicants must submit contact information for three referees.

References will receive automated requests to upload recommendation letters.

How to Apply

The application process begins with registration on the Online Fellowship Application Portal.

Step 1

Create an account using:

  • Name
  • Email address
  • Password

Step 2

Select one of the following application areas:

  • Humanities and Social Sciences
  • Creative Arts
  • Nonfiction and Journalism
  • Science, Engineering, and Mathematics

Step 3

Upload all required documents and submit your application before the deadline.

Application Deadlines

Applicants should carefully note the deadline that corresponds to their discipline.

Humanities, Social Sciences, Creative Arts, Nonfiction and Journalism

Deadline:

10 September 2026 at 5:00 PM ET

Science, Engineering, and Mathematics

Deadline:

1 October 2026 at 5:00 PM ET

Late submissions are generally not accepted.

Have Questions?

Applicants who need additional guidance can consult the Harvard Radcliffe Institute’s Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) section.

The FAQ covers:

Eligibility Questions

  • Am I eligible?
  • Can I apply if my project is interdisciplinary?
  • What career stages are accepted?

Application Materials

  • What should I submit as a work sample?
  • How should references be selected?
  • What should be included in the project proposal?

Review and Selection Process

  • How are applications evaluated?
  • What criteria are used?
  • How are interdisciplinary projects assessed?

After Submission

  • When will applicants receive updates?
  • What happens after submission?

Fellowship Logistics

  • What benefits do fellows receive?
  • Is relocation required?
  • What resources are available?

Applicants are strongly encouraged to review the FAQ before submitting questions, as it contains detailed guidance that can improve application quality and clarify requirements.

Why This Fellowship Matters

The Harvard Radcliffe Institute Fellowship Program has become a launching pad for groundbreaking research, influential scholarship, creative innovation, and public engagement.

At a time when societies face increasingly complex challenges—from scientific discovery and technological transformation to political polarization and social inequality—the need for interdisciplinary thinking has never been greater.

This fellowship provides participants with the rare opportunity to step back from daily obligations, immerse themselves in a community of exceptional thinkers, and produce work capable of influencing scholarship, public discourse, and policy.

Click HERE to learn more about the Harvard Radcliffe Institute Fellowship Program 2027–2028.

Conclusion

The Harvard Radcliffe Institute Fellowship Program 2027–2028 stands among the world’s most prestigious opportunities for scholars, scientists, writers, journalists, artists, and public intellectuals seeking to pursue ambitious projects within an intellectually rich and interdisciplinary environment. Through access to Harvard University’s extraordinary resources, collaboration with leading experts, and dedicated time for research and creative work, fellows can significantly advance projects that address some of society’s most pressing challenges.

For individuals with innovative ideas, transformative research agendas, or creative projects capable of making meaningful contributions to knowledge and public life, this fellowship offers an unparalleled platform for growth, impact, and intellectual exploration. Interested applicants should begin preparing their materials early and submit their applications before the relevant September or October 2026 deadlines.

Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on Opportunities for Youth (OFY).

The post Harvard Radcliffe Institute Fellowship Program 2027–2028: Prestigious Fully Funded Fellowship at Harvard University for Scholars, Scientists, Writers, Journalists, Artists, and Public Intellectuals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: L’Oréal USA Junior Associate – Corporate Brand & Engagement Internship 2026 in New York: Launch Your Career with the World’s Leading Beauty Company
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Are you a creative, digitally savvy, culturally aware, and ambitious student or recent graduate looking to build a career in marketing, communications, branding, public relations, or corporate affairs? L’Oréal USA is offering an exciting opportunity to join its Corporate Affairs & Engagement team as a Junior Associate – Corporate Brand & Engagement Intern in New […]

The post L’Oréal USA Junior Associate – Corporate Brand & Engagement Internship 2026 in New York: Launch Your Career with the World’s Leading Beauty Company appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a creative, digitally savvy, culturally aware, and ambitious student or recent graduate looking to build a career in marketing, communications, branding, public relations, or corporate affairs? L’Oréal USA is offering an exciting opportunity to join its Corporate Affairs & Engagement team as a Junior Associate – Corporate Brand & Engagement Intern in New York City.

This prestigious 12-month internship provides hands-on experience working on corporate branding initiatives, content creation, influencer engagement, strategic partnerships, advertising campaigns, and high-profile events for one of the most recognized beauty companies in the world.

Rather than observing from the sidelines, interns will become active contributors, helping shape how L’Oréal Groupe is positioned and perceived across the United States. This opportunity is ideal for individuals who are passionate about storytelling, culture, digital communication, marketing, and brand engagement.

About L’Oréal USA

L’Oréal USA is the largest subsidiary of L’Oréal Groupe, the world’s leading beauty company.

With more than 35 globally recognized brands and annual sales exceeding $7 billion, L’Oréal USA operates across numerous distribution channels including:

  • E-commerce platforms
  • Department stores
  • Hair salons
  • Pharmacies
  • Mass-market retailers
  • Medi-spas
  • Specialty beauty stores

The company employs more than 11,000 people across the United States and maintains research, manufacturing, distribution, and administrative facilities in 16 states.

L’Oréal’s continued success is driven by innovation, sustainability, creativity, and its commitment to the “L’Oréal for the Future” initiative, which focuses on responsible growth and sustainable development throughout its value chain.

Internship Overview

Position Details

  • Position Title: Junior Associate – Corporate Brand & Engagement
  • Internship Duration: 12 Months
  • Department: Corporate Affairs & Engagement
  • Division: Corporate
  • Location: New York, New York
  • Office: 10 Hudson Yards
  • Employment Type: Internship
  • Schedule: Full-Time (40 Hours Per Week)
  • Supervisor: Assistant Vice President, Head of US Corporate Brand & Engagement

This internship offers direct exposure to modern corporate communications and branding strategies while providing meaningful project ownership and professional development opportunities.

What You Will Learn

Interns will gain practical experience in several key areas of corporate communications and brand management, including:

Corporate Branding

Learn how one of the world’s largest beauty companies develops and maintains its corporate reputation through strategic communication initiatives.

Content Development

Gain experience creating content for:

  • Corporate websites
  • Social media platforms
  • Corporate events
  • Advertising campaigns
  • Internal and external communications

Corporate Communications

Understand how organizations communicate with stakeholders, media, consumers, and industry audiences.

Influencer Engagement

Learn how corporate influencers, thought leaders, and strategic partners help amplify brand messaging and corporate reputation.

Event Management

Develop event planning and execution skills through involvement in sponsorships, activations, and large-scale corporate events.

Campaign Analytics

Gain experience evaluating campaign performance through data collection, reporting, and analysis.

Internship Responsibilities

The Junior Associate will contribute across multiple functions within the Corporate Brand & Engagement team.

1. Content Creation and Corporate Advertising

Interns will help support content development and advertising initiatives designed to strengthen L’Oréal’s corporate brand presence.

Responsibilities include:

  • Brainstorming content concepts
  • Drafting copy for corporate communications
  • Supporting content creation for corporate channels
  • Assisting with social media content
  • Supporting live event content initiatives
  • Coordinating advertising assets
  • Tracking campaign performance
  • Managing agency deliverables

This role offers significant exposure to modern corporate storytelling and brand communication.

2. Events and Strategic Partnerships

Interns will assist with major corporate activations and sponsorship initiatives.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Researching venues
  • Supporting event logistics
  • Managing RSVP lists
  • Coordinating event assets
  • Distributing branding materials
  • Supporting sponsorship activities
  • Organizing gifting initiatives
  • Assisting with on-site event execution

This provides valuable experience in event management and partnership marketing.

3. Corporate Influencer Engagement

Influencer engagement has become an essential component of modern corporate communication.

Interns will:

  • Research potential partners and influencers
  • Identify relevant thought leaders
  • Assist with influencer collaborations
  • Monitor campaign coverage
  • Track engagement metrics
  • Measure campaign effectiveness
  • Compile performance reports

This experience provides insight into influencer relations and reputation management.

4. Administrative and Project Coordination

Interns will help keep projects organized and running efficiently.

Responsibilities include:

  • Coordinating calendars
  • Scheduling meetings
  • Supporting project management
  • Tracking budgets
  • Building presentations
  • Organizing digital assets
  • Managing file systems
  • Supporting team operations

These responsibilities help develop strong organizational and leadership skills.

Why This Internship Stands Out

Unlike many internship programs, this role places interns at the center of meaningful projects that directly contribute to corporate branding efforts.

Hands-On Experience

Interns are expected to actively contribute and take ownership of projects rather than simply observing.

Exposure to Industry Leaders

Work closely with experienced professionals responsible for shaping the corporate reputation of a globally recognized company.

Diverse Learning Opportunities

Gain exposure to:

  • Marketing
  • Corporate communications
  • Public relations
  • Digital media
  • Influencer marketing
  • Event planning
  • Brand management
  • Corporate affairs

Professional Portfolio Development

The projects completed during this internship can help build a strong professional portfolio for future career opportunities.

Networking Opportunities

Develop relationships with professionals across multiple business functions within one of the world’s largest beauty organizations.

Ideal Candidate Profile

L’Oréal is seeking candidates who are:

  • Creative thinkers
  • Strong communicators
  • Highly organized
  • Digitally fluent
  • Culturally aware
  • Curious and proactive
  • Collaborative team players
  • Passionate about branding and storytelling

The company values individuals who bring fresh perspectives while maintaining professionalism and attention to detail.

Educational Requirements

Applicants should meet one of the following criteria:

  • Recent graduate
  • Currently pursuing a degree

Preferred academic fields include:

  • Communications
  • Marketing
  • Public Relations
  • English
  • Media Studies
  • Journalism
  • Related disciplines

Required Skills and Qualifications

Communication Skills

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Excellent writing abilities
  • Strong verbal communication skills
  • Attention to grammar and detail
  • Effective storytelling skills

Social Media Knowledge

Candidates should be familiar with major digital platforms such as:

  • Instagram
  • TikTok
  • LinkedIn

A strong understanding of digital culture and online engagement trends is highly desirable.

Organizational Skills

Successful candidates will be:

  • Detail-oriented
  • Efficient multitaskers
  • Strong project managers
  • Reliable team members

Technical Skills

Applicants should possess proficiency in:

  • Microsoft Word
  • Microsoft PowerPoint
  • Microsoft Excel

Additional experience with the following is considered an advantage:

  • Canva
  • Adobe Creative Cloud
  • Social media management platforms

Industry Interest

A passion for or interest in the beauty industry is preferred.

Benefits of Joining L’Oréal USA

This internship offers numerous career-building advantages, including:

  • Experience with a globally recognized company
  • Exposure to executive-level communications
  • Hands-on branding and marketing experience
  • Influencer engagement experience
  • Event planning and sponsorship experience
  • Professional networking opportunities
  • Portfolio development
  • Career growth opportunities within communications and marketing

How to Apply

Click here for more and to apply

Interested candidates should apply through L’Oréal’s official careers platform.

Applicants should carefully review the role requirements before submitting an application and ensure they can commit to the full 12-month internship duration.

Because applications cannot be withdrawn once submitted, candidates are encouraged to prepare their application materials carefully before applying.

For aspiring communications professionals, marketers, storytellers, and brand strategists, the L’Oréal USA Junior Associate – Corporate Brand & Engagement Internship 2026 represents an outstanding opportunity to gain practical experience with one of the world’s most influential and innovative companies.

Click here for more global internship opportunities

The post L’Oréal USA Junior Associate – Corporate Brand & Engagement Internship 2026 in New York: Launch Your Career with the World’s Leading Beauty Company appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: WFP Internship 2026 in Rome, Italy: Fully Funded Opportunity to Gain Experience in Technology, Fundraising Operations, Salesforce, Data Management, and International Development
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The United Nations World Food Programme (WFP), the world’s largest humanitarian organization and recipient of the 2020 Nobel Peace Prize, has announced applications for the Tech Team Internship within the Private Sector Partnerships Division. This exciting opportunity offers aspiring technology and operations professionals the chance to work with one of the most respected international organizations […]

The post WFP Internship 2026 in Rome, Italy: Fully Funded Opportunity to Gain Experience in Technology, Fundraising Operations, Salesforce, Data Management, and International Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations World Food Programme (WFP), the world’s largest humanitarian organization and recipient of the 2020 Nobel Peace Prize, has announced applications for the Tech Team Internship within the Private Sector Partnerships Division. This exciting opportunity offers aspiring technology and operations professionals the chance to work with one of the most respected international organizations dedicated to fighting hunger and improving lives around the world.

The internship is based in Rome, Italy, with remote work arrangements available, providing successful candidates with exposure to international fundraising technologies, operational systems, data management processes, and digital transformation initiatives that support WFP’s global mission.

This internship is ideal for students and recent graduates who want to gain practical experience in technology, fundraising operations, digital platforms, data validation, reporting, and process improvement within a multicultural and international environment.

About the World Food Programme (WFP)

The World Food Programme is the largest humanitarian organization in the world, working to save lives during emergencies while helping communities build resilience and achieve long-term food security.

Operating in numerous countries worldwide, WFP provides emergency food assistance, supports sustainable development initiatives, and works with governments, donors, and private-sector partners to create pathways toward peace, stability, and prosperity.

As an organization committed to diversity and inclusion, WFP welcomes talented individuals from all backgrounds and cultures to contribute to its mission of achieving Zero Hunger globally.

Internship Overview

Position Title

Tech Team Intern

Organization

World Food Programme (WFP)

Division

Private Sector Partnerships (PSP)

Duty Station

Rome, Italy (Remote/Rome HQ)

Contract Type

Internship

Duration

8 Months

Application Deadline

28 June 2026 (23:59 GMT+01:00, Rome Time)

Why This Internship Matters

The Private Partnerships and Fundraising Division is focused on expanding fundraising efforts from private-sector partners and individual donors.

Individual giving has become one of the most successful fundraising channels used by international organizations because it:

  • Generates sustainable funding streams.
  • Provides flexible and unrestricted income.
  • Supports long-term strategic objectives.
  • Enables humanitarian organizations to respond rapidly to emergencies.
  • Strengthens organizational resilience and impact.

The Tech Team plays a crucial role in supporting these fundraising efforts through technology, digital platforms, operational systems, and data-driven processes.

As a Tech Team Intern, you will gain firsthand experience working at the intersection of:

  • Technology
  • Fundraising
  • Data Management
  • Digital Platforms
  • Reporting Systems
  • International Development
  • Humanitarian Operations

What Will the Intern Do?

The selected intern will work closely with the IG Tech Team Manager, developers, and other technical team members on a variety of operational and technical assignments.

1. Operational and Coordination Support

Interns will assist in managing recurring operational activities across the Tech Team.

Responsibilities include:

  • Supporting operational workflows.
  • Tracking tasks and deliverables.
  • Following up on ongoing activities.
  • Coordinating work across multiple teams.
  • Ensuring timely completion of assigned tasks.
  • Assisting with operational summaries and updates.
  • Supporting alignment across fundraising technology initiatives.

2. Systems and Platform Support

The internship offers practical exposure to several widely used professional platforms.

Interns will help support:

  • Salesforce
  • Microsoft 365
  • SharePoint
  • Asana

Key activities include:

  • Data uploads.
  • Platform monitoring.
  • Data validation exercises.
  • System checks.
  • User support activities.
  • Supporting fundraising technology operations.

3. Testing and Process Validation

A significant component of the internship involves quality assurance and system validation.

Interns will:

  • Participate in testing new platform features.
  • Evaluate system updates.
  • Validate operational processes.
  • Identify bugs and technical issues.
  • Report inconsistencies.
  • Verify reporting outputs.
  • Support continuous improvement initiatives.

4. Data and Reporting Support

Data quality is critical for effective fundraising operations.

The intern will help:

  • Maintain reporting trackers.
  • Validate recurring reports.
  • Monitor data flows.
  • Detect anomalies and inconsistencies.
  • Support reporting accuracy.
  • Prepare operational reports and summaries.

5. Documentation and Knowledge Management

Strong documentation practices are essential for efficient operations.

Interns will contribute by:

  • Updating internal documentation.
  • Maintaining operational guides.
  • Organizing SharePoint repositories.
  • Supporting knowledge-sharing initiatives.
  • Creating onboarding materials.
  • Improving documentation quality and accessibility.

6. Process Improvement Initiatives

The internship encourages innovation and continuous improvement.

Interns will:

  • Analyze existing workflows.
  • Suggest operational enhancements.
  • Improve reporting procedures.
  • Support implementation of improved practices.
  • Help optimize coordination and system usage.

Required Educational Qualifications

Applicants should possess a university degree in one of the following areas:

  • Information Technology
  • Computer Science
  • Information Systems
  • Business Administration
  • Related fields

Required Technical Competencies

Candidates should demonstrate:

  • Familiarity with Microsoft 365 applications.
  • Understanding of Outlook, Excel, Teams, and SharePoint.
  • Basic knowledge of Salesforce.
  • Familiarity with Asana or similar collaboration tools.
  • Understanding of data handling and reporting.
  • Experience maintaining records and trackers.
  • Ability to support testing and validation activities.

Essential Soft Skills

WFP seeks candidates who can demonstrate:

  • Strong communication skills.

  • Adaptability and willingness to learn.
  • Ability to manage multiple assignments.

  • Professionalism.
  • Collaborative mindset.

Language Requirement

Applicants must have:

WFP’s Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

WFP strongly promotes equal employment opportunities and welcomes applications from qualified individuals regardless of:

  • Race
  • Color
  • National origin
  • Ethnic background
  • Social background
  • Gender
  • Gender identity
  • Sexual orientation
  • Religion
  • Disability status
  • HIV status

The organization is committed to maintaining an inclusive workplace where diversity is valued and respected.

Application Process

Candidates should note the following:

  1. Applications must be submitted through WFP’s online recruitment platform.
  2. Applications sent by email will not be considered.
  3. Applicants should complete their Workday profile carefully.
  4. Only a CV and Cover Letter are required at the initial stage.
  5. Additional documents may be requested later.
  6. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Important Reminder

The United Nations never charges any fee during recruitment processes. Applicants should ignore any requests for payments related to applications, interviews, training, or recruitment activities.

Final Thoughts

The WFP Tech Team Internship 2026 presents an exceptional opportunity for aspiring technology and operations professionals seeking practical experience within the United Nations system. Through exposure to fundraising technologies, Salesforce platforms, reporting systems, data validation, and process improvement initiatives, interns will acquire highly valuable skills while contributing to a mission that impacts millions of lives worldwide.

For candidates interested in technology, digital transformation, data management, and humanitarian development, this internship offers a unique stepping stone toward an international career.

Application Deadline: 28 June 2026
